result.lens: Harmonic analysis on the motion group SE(2)
result.first_principles:
R^2's unit-distance graph is the orbit structure of the Gelfand pair (SE(2), SO(2)). Plane functions are the spherical (K-invariant) sector; every rotation-invariant positive-definite kernel is a J_0(lambda r) mixture. Two facts pin down exactly where Euclidean content can and cannot enter a harmonic-analytic lower bound.

(1) Collapse at 2 points. In the irrep pi_lambda on L^2(S^1), the unit step acts as multiplication by exp(i lambda cos phi); its matrix coefficients in the spin basis e_n are i^n J_n(lambda) e^{-in alpha} (Jacobi-Anger). The repo's Bochner kernel J_0 is literally the (0,0) entry of this noncommutative spectral data, the compression to the spherical vector e_0. Because E(2) acts vertex- AND edge-transitively on the plane UDG, the theta-type SDP has an invariant optimizer, so every 2-point Hoffman bound from any spin-weighted Hermitian edge kernel a(theta) is sandwiched below the radial value 1 + 1/0.402759 = 3.4829. The seed's hope is false at 2 points: noncommutativity adds nothing there. This is a load-bearing wall like L55's monoid: all SE(2) gain lives at >= 3 points.

(2) Where the off-diagonal J_n first act: 3 points. Graf's addition theorem, J_0(2 lambda sin(theta/2)) = sum_n J_n(lambda)^2 e^{in theta}, is exactly the resolution of identity sum_n |e_n><e_n| inserted between two unit steps with turn angle theta. So the abelian kernel evaluated on the third side of a (1,1,d) triangle is the diagonal SUM over spin channels. A coloring constrains each channel separately once color statistics are lifted to frames (x, theta) in SE(2): the stationary circle process of colors seen at unit distance around a common apex has PSD spin-Fourier blocks (matrix Herglotz), with properness zeros forced through the bridge at theta = pi/3 (unit equilateral triangle).

Detector alignment is exact. R^1: K trivial, no spin tower. L^infty: point group is finite D_4, no Bessel tower, and unit spheres meet in segments so the bridge theta -> d = 2 sin(theta/2) (a diffeomorphism by strict convexity, i.e. the two-intersection-point rigidity) degenerates. Q^2: no unit equilateral triangle exists (rational triangles have rational area, sqrt(3)/4 is not rational), so the pi/3 constraint is vacuous, on top of the measure-zero exemption.

conjectures.name: C1. Spherical collapse of 2-point SE(2) bounds
conjectures.statement: For every Hermitian kernel A(x,y) = a(arg(y-x)) supported on unit-distance pairs of R^2, with a in L^2(S^1) and a(theta+pi) = conj(a(theta)), the Hoffman chromatic value 1 + (ess sup s)/(|ess inf s|) of its Fourier symbol s(xi) = integral over theta of a(theta) exp(i xi . u_theta) satisfies the bound <= 1 + (sup J_0)/(|inf J_0|) = 3.48296, with equality iff a is constant (pure spin 0). Hence the abelian J_0 shadow is EXACTLY optimal among all SE(2)-covariant 2-point spectral bounds for chi_m(R^2), and every strict improvement must use >= 3-point structure.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Numerically maximize (sup of symbol)/(|inf of symbol|) over trigonometric-polynomial kernels a of degree <= 16, sampling lambda in [0, 60] and phi on a fine grid (the symbol is sum over n of c_n J_n(lambda) e^{in phi}). Prediction: the optimum is 2.4829 (= 0.402759^{-1}) attained with all mass on n = 0. Any kernel achieving ratio > 2.483 falsifies the conjecture (and immediately improves the plane's spectral chromatic bound, which would itself be notable).
conjectures.why_plausible: Three converging arguments: (a) for finite vertex- and edge-transitive graphs the best weighted ratio bound equals theta of the complement and is attained at the symmetric adjacency (Lovasz 1979); E(2) is edge-transitive on the plane UDG; (b) spin-n channels vanish at xi = 0 (J_n(0) = 0 for n != 0) so they cannot raise the sup above c_0, yet they average to zero over phi at every lambda, so they push the inf below c_0 min J_0; (c) the repo's L6 reproduces the radial value 0.287119 to 5e-7, and no published work has beaten 3.4829 by 2-point spectral means.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: The continuum sandwich needs care: the plane theta-analog involves essential spectra of convolution operators with continuous spectrum, and the finite-graph edge-transitivity theorem does not transfer verbatim. A strongly oscillatory complex a (odd-spin, chirality-carrying) could conceivably exploit the i^n phases to flatten the negative part of the symbol in a way my heuristic (b) misses.
conjectures.novelty_claim: The fact is folklore-adjacent for finite edge-transitive graphs, but its statement as 'all noncommutative SE(2) 2-point content for Hadwiger-Nelson collapses to the spherical J_0 compression' appears nowhere; it converts the Gelfand-pair structure into a negative result that localizes all possible gain at >= 3 points. Nearest prior art: Bachoc-Nebe-Oliveira-Vallentin 2009 (theta for distance graphs), Oliveira-Vallentin 2010 (the 0.287119 bound).

conjectures.name: C2. Graf-bridge spin-resolved triangle SDP crosses 4 (spectral re-proof of chi_m >= 5)
conjectures.statement: Define the frame-moment SDP for k colors: variables are (i) a nonnegative radial spectral measure mu with p(d) = integral J_0(lambda d) dmu(lambda), p(0) = 1, p(1) = 0, atom at lambda = 0 of mass >= 1/k; (ii) S_k-symmetrized apex-leg moments y_{c0,i,j}(theta) >= 0 for the configuration (x; x + u_alpha; x + u_{alpha+theta}), with properness zeros y = 0 when i = c0 or j = c0, leg marginals constant in theta, and Graf bridge sum_{c0,i} y_{c0,i,i}(theta) = p(2 sin(theta/2)) for all theta; (iii) matrix-Herglotz spin blocks: the Fourier coefficients hat B(n), |n| <= N, of B_{ij}(theta) = sum_{c0} y_{c0,i,j}(theta) are PSD. Conjecture: for k = 4 and N >= 8 this SDP is INFEASIBLE. That is a purely harmonic-analytic proof of chi_m(R^2) >= 5 with no finite-subgraph input, strictly past the abelian triangle ceiling (repo L9/e3c: 0.2619, i.e. 3.73) and past the 2-point cap 3.4829 of C1.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Implement in cvxpy (after S_k reduction the spin blocks become 2 scalar spectral inequalities per n, so this is nearly an LP): theta grid 720, lambda grid 160 points in (0.05, 45], N = 8. Sanity ladder: dropping spin blocks must reproduce 3.4829 (L6) and the abelian-triangle mode must reproduce 0.2619/3.73 (L9). Then: k = 4 infeasible (conjecture holds) or feasible (conjecture dies). If infeasible, extract the dual certificate and verify it on a 10x finer grid with mpmath interval Bessel evaluation. If k = 4 remains feasible at N = 16 and doubled grids, the conjecture is refuted and the negative result localizes Falconer's mechanism strictly above triangle order.
conjectures.why_plausible: Falconer's chi_m >= 5 argument is morally a (1,1,d)-configuration-plus-density argument that uses both intersection points of two unit circles; the spin blocks retain exactly the angle-resolved information the radialized LP integrates away. Graf's identity shows the abelian constraint is only the n-summed shadow of per-channel positivity; the per-channel constraints are genuinely additional unless a completion argument shows otherwise, and properness zeros at theta = pi/3 cut every diagonal channel simultaneously.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: A completion/averaging argument might show that at this order any abelian-feasible point extends to spin-feasible B(theta) (the spin constraints would then be implied, value stuck at 3.73). Discretization could fake infeasibility (mitigated by dual verification). Also p is only bridge-constrained for d <= 2; if the binding dual mass wants d > 2 information the order may be too low.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Partition-type (chromatic, joint over color classes) k-point bounds with SE(2) spin channels and the Graf addition theorem as the explicit bridge between the 2-point Bochner sector and the 3-point frame sector appear unstated. Nearest prior art: DeCorte-Oliveira-Vallentin complete-positivity hierarchy (density version, abelian presentation); Bachoc-Passuello-Thiery simplex-constrained theta; the repo's own e3c/L9 abelian triangle LP, which this strictly extends.

conjectures.name: C3. Achiral exclusion: measurable 5-colorings must be statistically chiral
conjectures.statement: In the frame-moment hierarchy, call a moment vector achiral if all odd-spin power vanishes (every functional of the moments quadratic in odd-n frame coefficients, e.g. order-4 terms |hat B(n)|^2 for odd n, is zero). Conjecture: there exist an order t and truncation N at which the k = 5 hierarchy restricted to achiral moment vectors is infeasible while the unrestricted k = 5 hierarchy at (t, N) is still feasible. Equivalently: if a measurable 5-coloring of R^2 exists, its unit-frame color statistics cannot be reflection-symmetric in the L^2 sense; chirality is a necessary resource exactly at the 4 -> 5 transition.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: At the C2 and C4 truncations, run the k = 5 instance twice: once with odd-spin power forced to 0, once unrestricted, and compare feasibility. Conjecture predicts a gap (achiral infeasible first). If both are feasible at all reachable orders the conjecture is unsupported; if both are infeasible, chi_m >= 6 outright, which supersedes it. A cheap independent check: verify the Isbell 7-coloring's frame moments are achiral (predicted yes) and compute odd-spin power for the best known 6-colorings of large regions (predicted nonzero).
conjectures.why_plausible: Three independent signals. Theorem R's clamp geometry is a chiral pair: two unit circles with distinct centers and their two intersection points; orientation is the only invariant of a point triple invisible to all 2-point analysis. The L^infty plane, which is reflection-rich and has no continuous spin structure, sits at chi = 4 exactly: the 4 -> 5 jump co-occurs with the availability of a continuous chirality invariant. And reflection-averaging kills odd FIRST moments but cannot kill odd POWER, so the statement lives precisely where convexity-based symmetrization arguments stop applying.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: If the hierarchy as implemented never couples odd-spin power into binding constraints (all constraints linear in moments, so reflection-averaging is WLOG at every order), the statement is vacuously false as posed and must be reformulated at the level of rank-constrained or exact (non-relaxed) moment sets. It is also possible that hypothetical 5-colorings are perfectly compatible with achiral statistics (e.g. some unknown reflection-symmetric near-5-coloring structure).
conjectures.novelty_claim: I am confident 'any measurable 5-coloring of the plane must have chiral frame statistics' has never been stated in the literature; I know of no prior art linking chirality to the Hadwiger-Nelson lower bound at all. Loose relatives: Voronov's map-type coloring results (orientation plays no explicit role there), and the L^infty chi = 4 theorem read as an achirality ceiling.
conjectures.wildness_note: wild, the genuinely new structural claim

conjectures.name: C4. Rhombus-order SE(2) hierarchy certifies chi_m(R^2) >= 6 and sidesteps the X_23 solver blocker
conjectures.statement: At the next hierarchy order, whose configurations are the Moser rhombus (two unit circles' centers plus BOTH intersection points: 4 points, 5 unit pairs, the minimal configuration carrying both chirality and two-intersection rigidity), the SE(2)-reduced frame-moment SDP for k = 5 is infeasible at spin truncation N <= 16, proving chi_m(R^2) >= 6. After exact SE(2) reduction the variables are functions of one radial frequency per spin pair (PSD blocks of size O(N x k-orbit count)), so the instance is orders of magnitude smaller than the blocked 48342-variable X_23 Lasserre: the finite graph X_23 BREAKS O(2) and pays for it in variables; the continuum keeps O(2) exact and the reduction is total.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Feasibility run of the k = 5 rhombus-order instance (cvxpy scale by construction). Secondary quantitative prediction: the infeasibility certificate's radial measure concentrates its mass in lambda in [2, 8], around j_{0,1} = 2.405 and j_{1,1} = 3.832 (the repo's known optimal frequency band). Refuted if the SDP is feasible at N = 16 with fine grids, or (weaker form) if its bound stays below 5.
conjectures.why_plausible: The rhombus is exactly the geometry of Falconer's argument and of Theorem R's clamp interface; every known jump in plane bounds (Moser spindle, de Grey's H-machinery, the W3 clamp criterion) routes through two unit circles meeting in exactly two points. C1 proves the gain cannot be at 2 points; L9 shows abelian 3-point caps at 3.73; the program's remaining mass therefore sits on spin-resolved 3-4 point structure, and the rhombus is the first order where all of it is present simultaneously.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: k-point hierarchies converge to chi_m only asymptotically (DOV), and the rhombus order may cap anywhere in (4, 6): a value in (4, 5] would still be the best spectral bound known but refute the conjecture. If de Grey's omega <= 3 world is right and chi = 5 with a 5-coloring whose moments are very flat, low orders may stay feasible forever. A subtle failure: the bridge constraints for the rhombus require p on d in (0, 2] only, and binding information might genuinely need longer-range correlations.
conjectures.novelty_claim: No SDP route to chi_m >= 6 exists in the literature (current beyond-Falconer work is subgraph-based or the repo's blocked finite Lasserre). The specific claim that the continuum SE(2) reduction makes the order-2 relaxation tractable where the finite-graph version is not appears to be new. Nearest prior art: the repo's X_23 order-2 attack (blocker 5) and the DOV hierarchy framework.

experiment.description: Build experiments/measurable/e2d_se2_spin_triangle_sdp.py implementing the C2 instance. Variables after S_k reduction: two orbit functions of theta on a 720-point grid (apex-leg moments with equal vs distinct leg colors), a nonnegative radial measure on a 160-point lambda grid in (0.05, 45], and the unit-pair density scalars. Constraints: Graf bridge sum_{c0,i} y_{c0,i,i}(theta) = p(2 sin(theta/2)) with p(d) = sum_j mu_j J_0(lambda_j d) (closed form via scipy.special.jn, no numerical integration); p(0) = 1, p(1) = 0, DC atom >= 1/k; leg marginals constant in theta; properness zeros; matrix-Herglotz spin blocks hat B(n) PSD for |n| <= 8, which under S_k reduce to two scalar spectral inequalities per n (near-LP). Protocol: (step 0) sanity: spin blocks off must reproduce 0.287119/3.4829 (repo L6) and abelian-triangle mode must reproduce 0.2619/3.73 (repo L9); (step 1) k = 3 must be infeasible (since 3.4829 > 3); (step 2) THE decisive bit: k = 4 feasibility. Infeasible: C2 confirmed, extract dual certificate, re-verify on a 10x finer grid with mpmath interval Bessel, cache under experiments/measurable/_cache/, then escalate to C4. Feasible at N = 16 and doubled grids: C2 dead, which cleanly localizes Falconer's mechanism above triangle order and redirects everything to the rhombus order before any C4 investment. Either branch is informative; that is what makes this the decisive experiment.
experiment.repo_feasibility: Entirely within the existing stack: cvxpy (Clarabel/SCS) or HiGHS for the near-LP form, scipy.special for J_n, mpmath for interval verification, no C compiler needed, no SAT. Reuses the e3b/e3c Bessel-LP scaffolding already validated in L6/L9. Certificate caching follows existing _cache conventions. The S_k reduction is 20 lines of sympy-checked algebra (B(theta) = b1 I + b2 (J - I); spectral channels b1 - b2 and b1 + (k-1) b2).
experiment.est_cost: Roughly 300-400 lines of Python, about one day of work; each solve is seconds to minutes on this host; full protocol including dual verification well under one session.

detector_self_check.Q2: The method is measure-theoretic (Architecture 2 partial exemption: densities and stationary frame processes do not exist on a null countable set). But there is also a structural pass beyond the exemption: the engine's load-bearing constraint is the theta = pi/3 properness zero transmitted through the Graf bridge, i.e. the unit equilateral triangle. Q^2 contains no unit equilateral triangle (a triangle with rational vertices has rational area; side-1 equilateral area is sqrt(3)/4), so on Q^2 the constraint set is vacuous and the bound machinery proves nothing. The argument cannot lift to chi(Q^2) >= 3.
detector_self_check.Linf: Two Euclidean-specific inputs are load-bearing and both fail in L^infty. First, the spin tower: the bound uses the infinite family of PSD spin blocks hat B(n) coming from the continuous rotation group SO(2) acting freely and transitively on unit directions; the L^infty point group is the finite dihedral D_4, so only 4 characters exist and there is no Bessel tower or Graf identity (no Gelfand-pair Bessel structure for the square norm). Second, the bridge theta -> d = 2 sin(theta/2) is a diffeomorphism precisely because the Euclidean circle is strictly convex (two unit spheres meet in <= 2 points); L^infty unit spheres meet in segments, so the (1,1,d) configuration space is degenerate and the properness zeros do not propagate through any analog bridge. Predicted transplant value in L^infty: <= 4, consistent with Chilakamarri; this is itself checkable on a discretized analog and is listed as an adversarial test.
detector_self_check.R1: On the line, K = SO(1) is trivial: frames carry only a Z_2 direction bit, the spin tower collapses to two characters, Graf degenerates to the cosine addition formula, and the (1,1,d) triangle moduli are the two points d in {0, 2} with no properness content (d = 2 is a non-edge). The SDP then reduces to the abelian 1D Bochner bound with symbol cos(xi), giving exactly chi >= 2. The rotation group is explicitly the load-bearing object (the entire construction is harmonic analysis OF the rotation-translation interplay), so the method is not rotation-blind and does not over-constrain chi(R^1).
